message:
feat: add structured JSON tracing to fcp-host binary and verify
endpoint log events in integration tests

Wire up tracing-subscriber in the fcp-host binary so that every HTTP
endpoint handler emits structured JSON log lines to stderr, then add
integration test infrastructure to capture and assert on those logs.

crates/fcp-host/Cargo.toml:
- Add tracing-subscriber dependency with env-filter and json features

crates/fcp-host/src/bin/fcp-host.rs:
- New init_tracing() function: configures a tracing-subscriber registry
  with an EnvFilter (defaults to info,fcp_host=debug) and a JSON
  formatter that writes flattened events to stderr with ANSI disabled
  and current-span tracking off
- main() now calls init_tracing() before entering the async runtime

crates/fcp-host/tests/host_connector_integration.rs:
- New spawn_stderr_capture(child) helper: takes the child process's
  stderr pipe, spawns a background thread that reads lines into an
  Arc<StdMutex<Vec<String>>>, returns the shared log buffer and
  JoinHandle for cleanup
- New wait_for_log_events(stderr_logs, events) helper: polls the
  captured log buffer up to a 2-second deadline, parsing each line as
  JSON and checking for the presence of all expected event names;
  returns the full parsed log vector on success
- HttpHostProcess and UnixHostProcess now carry stderr_logs and
  stderr_thread fields, integrated into spawn() and Drop
- New test fcp_host_binary_emits_structured_endpoint_logs: spawns two
  test connectors, exercises discover/introspect/preflight/doctor/
  health endpoints, then asserts on structured log fields including
  connector_count, registry_version, connector_id, tool_count,
  operation, allowed, overall_status

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
